tool_args.label: NYCT, SIR, and MTA Bus Financial & Ridership Reports – January 2023

payload.title: New York City Transit Financial and Ridership Reports – January 2023
payload.description: Financial and ridership reports for NYCT, Staten Island Railway, and MTA Bus Company for January 2023, including budget vs actual performance, headcount, and variance explanations.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

NYCT Bus Farebox Revenue Underrun Cause

payload.claim_text: NYCT Bus farebox revenue was $3.4 million (6.3%) lower than the Budget entirely due to lower than projected ridership.

tool_args.label: NYCT Labor Expense Favorable Variance Cause

payload.claim_text: Labor expenses were $13.7 million (2.2%) favorable primarily due to vacancies.

tool_args.label: SIR Track Replacement Material Usage

payload.claim_text: Materials and Supplies for SIR were $0.200 million unfavorable primarily due to the usage of rails for the Track replacement program.

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Farebox Revenue Variance Cause

payload.claim_text: MTA Bus Company farebox revenue was $0.5 million (3.9%) unfavorable due to lower average fare offset by higher ridership.

tool_args.label: NYCT Ridership Increase vs 2022

payload.claim_text: Total NYCT ridership was 118.1 million, which was 35.1% greater than 2022

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Company Pension Prepayment

payload.claim_text: MTA Bus Company pension variance on cash basis was ($117.544 million) primarily due to prepayment of 2023 and 2024 Pension Expense.

tool_args.label: NYCT Vacancies Description

payload.claim_text: The largest number of vacancies was in Subways/Bus Maintenance (1,495 positions) and Subways Operations (748 positions).

tool_args.label: Results Based on Preliminary Close

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. Please note actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.
payload.description: All actual results in this report are preliminary and subject to post-close adjustments.

tool_args.label: Totals May Not Add Due to Rounding

payload.gap_text: Totals may not add due to rounding
payload.description: Financial statement totals may not sum exactly due to rounding.

tool_args.label: NYCT report published by MTA

payload.description: Financial report prepared by Jaibala Patel, Deputy Chief Financial Officer, MTA

tool_args.label: NYCT has metric: total revenue
NYCT has metric: total ridership
SIR has metric: total ridership
MTA Bus Company has metric: total ridership
NYCT Overtime Variance Cause

payload.claim_text: NYCT Overtime was $2.7 million (6.1%) unfavorable mainly due to vacancy / availability back-fill coverage and adverse weather response.

tool_args.label: NYCT Fuel Variance Cause

payload.claim_text: NYCT Fuel was $4.4 million (36.5%) unfavorable primarily due to higher price and unfavorable timing of diesel fuel charges.

tool_args.label: NYCT Pension Prepayment Cash Variance

payload.claim_text: NYCT Pension variance on cash basis was ($318.7 million) (434.8%) due to prepaid two years of pension expense.

tool_args.label: SIR Pension Prepayment Cash Variance

payload.claim_text: SIR Pension cash variance of ($15.806 million) due to pre-payment of two years pension expenses.

tool_args.label: NYCT Payroll Favorable Cause

payload.claim_text: NYCT Payroll was $16.9 million (5.3%) favorable primarily due to vacancies.

tool_args.label: NYCT Electric Power Favorable Cause

payload.claim_text: NYCT Electric Power was favorable by $3.4 million (8.6%) due to lower consumption and timing.
